$schema_note: GUARD B — RETIRED-DOCTRINE RESIDUAL LINT registry. SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for doctrine VALUES that have been retired and must never silently re-appear in the Presentations tree. scripts/doctrine_residual_check.py greps each entry's `pattern` (regex, IGNORECASE+MULTILINE) over the files matched by `scope` (glob, relative to the presentations/ root). Any match on a line/block that does NOT also carry an allowed_context_marker (an explicit retirement/replacement signal) is an OFFENDER and fails the check non-zero. A retired value is allowed to survive ONLY as documented history — i.e. on a line (or within `context_window` lines) that says it was retired/replaced. RETIRING A DOCTRINE VALUE IS A CONTRACT: add it here (Guard B) so the OLD value can never quietly creep back in as a live instruction. To add a new retired pattern: append an object with a unique `id`, a `pattern` that matches the retired value, a `scope` glob, a one-line `why`, and (optionally) per-pattern `allowed_context_markers` (otherwise the registry-level defaults apply).

patterns.why: The retired '>= 7 / sung at least 7 times / seven times' HOOK FLOOR produced the reference-case 40-slide footer-stamping. The live gate is the banded AF-HOOK CEILING (3-4 dedicated slides). The old floor must never re-appear as a live instruction.

FIX-PRES-09(v): the presentations image-prompt floor was RAISED from 5,000 chars to 9,000 chars (current HARD floor 9,000, ceiling 18,000; build_deck.py PROMPT_CHAR_FLOOR=9000, AF-P1). The retired 5,000-char floor must never re-appear as a live instruction — a prompt authored to 5,000 chars is now sub-floor and is refused, not run.

P3-01(c)6 — the doctrine schism: dozens of role/SOP files cited a numbered "master SOP Section N.N" that no longer exists in any single file (the monolithic master SOP was decomposed into the universal-sops/ + sops/ SOP-* cluster). universal-sops/PRESENTATION-MASTER-DOCTRINE.md Section 4 is the ratified crosswalk from every legacy section number to its live home; every role/SOP file was repointed to cite the live file directly. The bare 'master SOP Section N' / 'CLIENT-WEBINAR-DECK-SOP.md (Section N' citation form must never re-appear — it points into a void.

P3-01(c)6 / PRESENTATION-MASTER-DOCTRINE.md Section 1 (THE HANDSHAKE BETWEEN THE LAYERS): 'The retired claim that the script composes the KIE prompt mechanically is FALSE for the current engine and is a banned residual pattern.' build_deck.py renders the Layer-A-authored 9,000-18,000-char rich per-slide prompt VERBATIM; it does not compose a prompt from a bare scene+copy pair. This claim must never re-appear as a live instruction in BUILDER-PROMPT.md / SOUL.md / TOOLS.md or any role file.
